product_brief: Platform that discovers, protects, and automates every certificate across any CA, cloud, and machine.
pqc_capability_description: Supports discovery of post-quantum (PQ) and hybrid certificates via real-time visibility. Offers a "PQC Lab" sandbox for quantum readiness and resources. Mentions "Quantum-ready algorithms" and Key Encapsulation Mechanisms (KEMs) in educational content, but specific algorithm names are not listed in the product feature text.
crypto_primitives: Not stated (Text mentions "hybrid certificates", "PQ certificates", and "Key Encapsulation Mechanisms" generally, but does not list specific primitives like ECDSA, Ed25519, or specific PQC algorithms).
key_management_model: On-device key generation; integrations to popular HSMs and key vaults. Mentions "four-eye / dual-person control for protecting your keys on the HSM".
architecture_type: Centralized governance with modular orchestrators; supports SaaS, On-Premises (Self-hosted), Kubernetes (Helm Charts), and Hybrid (CLA as a Service + Managed PKI).
license: Not stated (Text mentions "Community editions" of EJBCA and SignServer are open source, but does not specify the license name like Apache-2.0; Keyfactor Command is commercial).
fips_validated: Partial (Bouncy Castle APIs are described as "FIPS-Certified"; HSM FIPS mode is discussed in educational content, but no specific validation statement for Keyfactor Command itself).
primary_platforms: On-premises data centers, Cloud (Azure mentioned for SaaS Lite), Kubernetes clusters.
target_industries: Financial Services, Telecom, Automotive, Medical, Industrial IoT, Consumer IoT, Software & IT.
pqc_roadmap_details: Offers "PQC Lab" sandbox and resources; focuses on "Achieve Crypto-Agility" and "Quantum Readiness"; specific timelines or algorithm migration schedules are not stated.
